channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Airbus unveils U145 autonomous helicopter drone for cargo supply ops | Breaking Defense
item.description: Other military roles for the drone cover armed scouting, surveillance, crewed-uncrewed teaming as well as acting as a “mothership” for air launched effects, according to the European prime.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 8, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated guide to major news and developments over the weekend. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs yesterday, in retaliation for a Hezbollah missile attack on northern Israel,…

item.title: 🌏 The U.S. Can’t Exclude China From Latin America | Foreign Policy
item.description: Rather than pushing Beijing out of the region, Washington should offer countries better strategic alternatives.

item.title: 🏛️ French Army has recruitment surplus but lacks equipment, deputy chief says | Defense News
item.description: “This year, we’re already slowing down recruitment because not only are we recruiting better, but we’re retaining people better," a service leader said.

item.title: 🌍 Palladyne AI partners with IAI to bring Israeli Harpy, Harop drones to US | Breaking Defense
item.description: Palladyne plans to put new AI swarming software on the battle-proven Israeli drones and compete for contracts like the Army’s Long-Range Precision Munition (LRPM), CEO Ben Wolff told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 📍 Thailand Says It Will Join UN-Backed Conciliation on Maritime Dispute With Cambodia | The Diplomat
item.description: Thai Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said that Bangkok will cease all other bilateral talks while mediation is in progress.

item.title: 🏛️ The Lawmakers Fighting to Modernize the Pentagon | War on the Rocks
item.description: Congress rarely moves fast, but Reps. Rob Wittman and Pat Ryan are trying to change that. The two lawmakers founded the bipartisan House Defense Modernization Caucus in 2024 and have driven reforms through two consecutive defense authorization acts, targeting acquisitions and…

item.title: 📍 What Beirut’s Port Scanners Miss About Militant Supply Chains | War on the Rocks
item.description: At the Port of Beirut, the new scanners did exactly what they were built to do. They saw the lithium batteries. They saw the drone propellers. They saw the fiber optic cable. They matched the scans against the paperwork, found no obvious deception, and cleared the cargo.That was…

item.title: 📍 Propaganda and Fake News Editors: Myanmar’s Manufactured Transition | The Diplomat
item.description: Min Aung Hlaing's “civilian” government rests on a sophisticated propaganda architecture, and the independent media needed to challenge it is running out of funds.

item.title: ⚛️ Macron’s nuclear pact expands across Scandinavia as global forces surges | Breaking Defense
item.description: Norway is just the latest ally to open discussions with Paris about France’s extended nuclear deterrence initiative.

item.title: 📍 Trump Is Doing What FDR Could Not | Foreign Policy
item.description: But the president’s success at purging his party could cost the GOP in the long term.

item.title: 📍 Greeted as Liberators? | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How Americans think about U.S. military interventions.

item.title: 📍 The Day After in Cuba | Foreign Affairs
item.description: What American military force can and cannot do.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Launches Ballistic Missile Attacks On Israel (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The possibility of open conflict once again between Iran and Israel rose after Tehran struck in response to the IDF bombing Beirut on Sunday, The post Iran Launches Ballistic Missile Attacks On Israel (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ House lawmakers want the Navy to deploy drone boats faster | Defense One
item.description: HASC’s draft of the 2027 defense policy bill would push service leaders to develop a clear strategy.

item.title: 📍 F-47’s Exotic Shape Was Hiding In Plain Sight On A Unit Patch | The War Zone
item.description: Boeing's NGAD demonstrator's unique planform was hinted at on a patch, just like its progenitor, the Bird of Prey. The post F-47’s Exotic Shape Was Hiding In Plain Sight On A Unit Patch appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Charai for Newsmax: No Trust, No Illusions, No Nuclear Iran | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for Newsmax: No Trust, No Illusions, No Nuclear Iran appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 A-10 Cockpit And Walk-Around Tour With A Warthog Weapons Instructor | The War Zone
item.description: We get up-close and personal with the Warthog on the flightline at Nellis AFB. The post A-10 Cockpit And Walk-Around Tour With A Warthog Weapons Instructor appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Under Trump 2.0, the Quad’s Real Challenge to China Is Economic, Not Military | The Diplomat
item.description: The grouping is unlikely to harden into an “Asian NATO.” For Beijing, the more consequential contest is over supply chains, technology standards, and the rules of the Indo-Pacific economy.

item.title: 🌏 North Korea’s Posturing Toward China Ahead of Xi’s Visit | 38 North
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping’s upcoming state visit to North Korea—his first since June 2019 ... The post North Korea’s Posturing Toward China Ahead of Xi’s Visit appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🌍 US strikes Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up | Defense News
item.description: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said.

item.title: 🏴 France to test its own AI-powered battlefield command in June NATO exercise | Defense News
item.description: The French Army has developed its own large-language model for staff officers, called Berthier, named after Napoleon’s chief of staff.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Shoots Down Iranian Drones Launched At Strait Of Hormuz: Official (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The incident is the latest flare-up between Iran and the U.S. since the war was put on hold nearly two months ago. The post U.S. Shoots Down Iranian Drones Launched At Strait Of Hormuz: Official (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Armenians Vote Under Russia’s Shadow | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump and Putin are backing different players in a contested country.

item.title: 🏛️ Confessions Of A Navy MH-53E Sea Dragon Minehunter Pilot | The War Zone
item.description: Former Sea Dragon pilot Steve Jones offers unique insights into the challenges of flying around the Persian Gulf searching for mines. The post Confessions Of A Navy MH-53E Sea Dragon Minehunter Pilot appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: When will it be time to go outside? The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Area 51 Mystery Aircraft Prompts Interest In “Christmas Tree” Stealth Fighter Concept | The War Zone
item.description: The exotic jet spotted near Area 51 that appears to be tied to the F-47 has prompted a look at an obscure fighter design from the 1980s. The post Area 51 Mystery Aircraft Prompts Interest In “Christmas Tree” Stealth Fighter Concept appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 USAF Scrambling To Buy What Few MQ-9 Reapers It Can Find After Epic Fury Losses | The War Zone
item.description: The USAF needs to scrounge-up any now out of production Reapers to replace the dozens lost in fights against Iran and the Houthis. The post USAF Scrambling To Buy What Few MQ-9 Reapers It Can Find After Epic Fury Losses appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The U.S. Economy Is Proving Remarkably Resilient | Foreign Policy
item.description: Even in the face of tariffs and an energy crisis, the jobs report shows an economy that’s still humming.

item.title: 📍 The global energy crisis and its impacts on Asian emerging economies | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz energy crisis poses a stress test for import-dependent, emerging economies in Asia, forcing difficult trade-offs. The post The global energy crisis and its impacts on Asian emerging economies appeared first on Atlantic Council.
